_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/COVID-19 infection/ : Treatment of COVID-19 infection_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Treatment of COVID-19 infection_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Moderate clinical severity defined by clinical features of dyspnea and or hypoxia, fever, cough, SpO2 <94% (90-94%) on room air, or respiratory rate >/= 24 per minute.
Severe clinical severity presenting as Severe Pneumonia, Acute Respiratory Distress Syndrome (ARDS), Sepsis, or Septic shock.

preauth.label: Clinical notes including history, signs & symptoms
Laboratory Investigation report (Real time or Conventional RT-PCR test)
Report of upper respiratory tract samples (Throat/Nasal/Nasopharyngeal swab & Oropharyngeal swab)
Report of lower respiratory tract samples (Bronchoalveolar lavage/tracheal aspirate/sputum) (when readily available for mechanically ventilated patients)
Test reports for other potential etiologies (e.g. Influenza, other respiratory viruses, malaria, dengue fever, typhoid fever) (as appropriate)
Blood culture report (for severe COVID-19 patients, prior to initiation of antimicrobial therapy)

claim.label: Detailed Indoor case papers (treatment, daily monitoring of temperature, vitals, SpO2; Chest X-ray & CBC in moderate/severe illness; supportive therapy)
Detailed discharge summary with post-discharge advise
Negative RT-PCR test report (for severe cases, once after symptom resolution, before patient discharge)

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes detail history, risk factors, signs, symptoms, vitals, investigations, and admission criteria?
Photograph of the patient on bed available?
Patient presents with signs/symptoms of mild/moderate/severe COVID-19 infection?
Laboratory report confirms COVID-19 infection at admission?

cpd_claim.q: Documents (clinical notes, physical exam, lab reports) detail the need for admission?
Documentary evidence of vital monitoring (SpO2, Heart rate, chest exam) available?
Medication/treatment chart available and treatment aligns with MoH&FW COVID-19 protocol?
Discharge documents clearly indicate discharge criteria as per MoH&FW revised policy for COVID-19?

common_queries: Laboratory RT-PCR test report not provided or does not confirm COVID-19 infection at admission.
Clinical notes lack sufficient detail on patient history, signs, symptoms, vitals, or admission criteria.
Planned line of management is not documented or is unclear at pre-authorization.
Photograph of the patient on bed is missing or insufficient for identity verification.
Discharge summary does not clearly state discharge criteria met as per MoH&FW revised policy.
Vital monitoring records (temperature, SpO2, heart rate, chest examination) are incomplete or missing in indoor case papers.
Negative RT-PCR test report for discharge (for severe cases) is missing.

additional_information.special_empanelment: ICMR/Government approved treatment facility requirement, Oximeter, and other requirements.
additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/ DNB/ equivalent in Medicine/ Pulmonology/ Diploma in Tuberculosis and Chest Disease (DTCD)
clinical_key_pointers: Suspect case definition as per MoH&FW guidelines.
Probable case definition as per MoH&FW guidelines.
Confirmed case: laboratory confirmation of COVID-19 infection, irrespective of clinical signs and symptoms.
Common signs & symptoms include fever, cough, fatigue, shortness of breath, expectoration, myalgia, rhinorrhea, sore throat, diarrhea, loss of smell (anosmia), loss of taste (ageusia).

Risk factors for severe disease include age > 60 years and underlying non-communicable diseases (diabetes, hypertension, cardiac disease, chronic lung disease, cerebro-vascular disease, chronic kidney disease, immune-suppression, cancer).
Admission criteria: Moderate clinical severity (dyspnea, hypoxia, SpO2 <94% (90-94%), respiratory rate >= 24/min) or Severe clinical severity (Severe Pneumonia, ARDS, Sepsis, Septic shock).
Mild clinical severity cases (normal oxygen saturation) are preferably managed at home.
Discharge criteria are as per extant MoH&FW Guidelines.
